BestofJuly 6, 2026

7 Best AI Monitoring and Observability Tools for 2026

Monitor your LLM applications with confidence. These AI observability tools track costs, performance, and quality in production.

A

AI Tools Team

Published July 6, 2026

Featured Tools (7)

#1

LangSmith

Best for LangChain

LangChain's official observability platform — trace, debug, and evaluate LLM applications.

#2

Langfuse

Best Open Source

Open-source LLM observability with self-hosting options and generous free tier.

#3

Helicone

Easiest Setup

Simple, powerful LLM analytics — one line of code to start monitoring.

#4

Arize Phoenix

Best for ML Teams

Full-stack ML observability for tracing, evaluation, and troubleshooting.

#5

Weights & Biases

Best Experiments

The industry standard for ML experiment tracking, now with LLM monitoring.

#6

Braintrust

Best for Eval

AI product development platform with built-in evals and monitoring.

#7

Patronus AI

Best for Safety

AI safety and quality monitoring — detect hallucinations and issues in production.

Building an AI application is only half the battle. Monitoring it in production — tracking costs, catching errors, and ensuring quality — is where the real challenge begins. These tools make it manageable.

Why You Need LLM Monitoring

Without observability, your AI application is a black box. You won’t know:

  • How much it costs — Token usage and API spend can spiral quickly
  • When it fails — Hallucinations and errors happen silently
  • How users interact — Which prompts work, which ones don’t
  • How quality changes — Model updates can break your app

The Best AI Monitoring Tools

1. LangSmith — Best for LangChain

If you’re using LangChain, LangSmith is the natural choice. It provides detailed traces of your chains, agents, and retrievers. Debugging complex LLM workflows is dramatically easier with LangSmith.

Pricing: Free tier, then $39/month for individuals.

2. Langfuse — Best Open Source

Langfuse is the leading open-source LLM observability tool. You can self-host it for free or use their cloud service. It supports LangChain, LlamaIndex, and direct API calls.

Pricing: Free (self-hosted), cloud plans from $59/month.

3. Helicone — Easiest Setup

Helicone requires just one line of code to start monitoring. It tracks costs, latency, and usage across all major LLM providers. The setup is so simple it’s almost trivial.

Pricing: Free tier with generous limits, then $20/month.

4. Arize Phoenix — Best for ML Teams

Phoenix provides full-stack observability for LLM applications. It’s particularly strong for teams that need to trace issues across multiple components.

Pricing: Free (open source), enterprise plans available.

5. Weights & Biases — Best Experiments

W&B is the industry standard for ML experiment tracking. Their LLM monitoring features extend this to production applications, with excellent visualization and comparison tools.

Pricing: Free for individuals, team plans from $50/month.

6. Braintrust — Best for Evaluation

Braintrust focuses on AI product development with built-in evaluation tools. It helps you systematically test and improve your LLM applications.

Pricing: Free tier, usage-based pricing.

7. Patronus AI — Best for Safety

Patronus specializes in AI safety monitoring. It detects hallucinations, bias, and quality issues in production — essential for applications where accuracy matters.

Pricing: Custom enterprise pricing.

AFFILIATE_LINK

FAQ

Do I need LLM monitoring?

If you’re running any LLM in production, yes. Without monitoring, you can’t track costs, detect failures, or improve quality.

Which tool is best for beginners?

Helicone is the easiest to set up — one line of code and you’re monitoring. Langfuse is the best open-source option.

Can I self-host these tools?

Langfuse and Arize Phoenix are fully open-source and can be self-hosted. Most others require their cloud service.

How much does LLM monitoring cost?

Most tools offer free tiers that are sufficient for small projects. Production plans typically cost $20-100/month depending on traffic.
